name: Signal Event Rate Heuristic Registry
summary: Family-local interpretation of shared heuristics for waveform to event to rate pipelines.
dejargonized_summary: This registry says which generic warning signs matter most for pipelines that turn a sampled waveform into detected events and then a derived rate.

admissibility_notes: Prefer cleanup before the first major transformation when abrupt input steps are present.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when discontinuities occur before the first conditioning stage.
family_notes: In signal families, this often means preserving the waveform path long enough to remove abrupt discontinuities before the main detector-facing transform.

admissibility_notes: Quality variation should not silently force one brittle path when a gate or alternate branch is available.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when local quality changes enough that one fixed path is likely to fail on part of the window.
family_notes: The shared heuristic stays generic; the family note explains the waveform-specific reading.

admissibility_notes: Downstream measure stages should not trust unstable event spacing without cleanup or smoothing.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when implausible event spacing persists after an otherwise valid detector path.
family_notes: This is intentionally phrased as interval instability rather than a biological term.

audit.rationale: Captures family-local interpretation of shared heuristics without redefining their canonical meaning.
audit.dejargonized_summary: These are the generic warning signs that matter most for waveform-to-rate pipelines and the kinds of structural responses they support.
uncertainty_notes: Family guidance should remain local and should not redefine the shared heuristic identifiers.

references.title: Signal event rate heuristic registry
references.citation: Local transitional family registry for shared heuristic interpretation.
